name: Prime Marble and Granite Ltd
description: Prime Marble and Granite is a Zambian dimension stone quarrying and processing company listed on the LuSE Alternative Market (ALT-M) since 2021. The company quarries, cuts, and supplies marble and granite for the construction and monument industries in Zambia and potentially for export. As a small-cap industrial with ALT-M listing, the company operates under lighter disclosure and regulatory requirements than the Main Board.

financials.note: NO PUBLICLY AVAILABLE FINANCIAL DATA FOUND. ALT-M companies have minimal web presence and lighter disclosure obligations. The company may file abridged results with LuSE SENS but these were not indexed by search engines. Note: Search results returned 'Zambia Reinsurance PLC (formerly Prima Reinsurance)' which is a DIFFERENT company — not to be confused with Prime Marble and Granite (PRIM).

ratios.peNote: From companies.json — source and calculation basis unverified
ratios.dividendNote: From companies.json — 1% yield unverified. With zero recorded trading volume (companies.json), price discovery is suspect.

ownership.note: 55% free float is unusually high for ALT-M — could indicate dispersed founding shareholders rather than institutional float

analystNotes.note: PRIM is a micro-cap ALT-M listed quarrying company with zero recorded trading volume (per companies.json) and no publicly accessible financial data. At ZMW 4.80/share with a ZMW 290M market cap and zero volume, this is a listed company in name only — there is no functioning secondary market. The 55% free float is theoretical; in practice, liquidity is non-existent. The company may have real quarrying operations, but as an investable security, it does not function. FLAGGED FOR EVIDENCE GATHERING.
analystNotes.author: Vituli Musukuma, PhD

riskFactors: Zero recorded trading volume — no functioning market
ALT-M micro-cap with minimal regulatory oversight
55% free float is theoretical — practical liquidity is zero
investmentThesis: There is no investment thesis for PRIM. A stock with zero trading volume is not a stock — it's a private company with a listing certificate. The business may be viable as a private quarrying operation, but as a public market security, it is non-functional.
dataQuality: VERY LOW — No audited financials accessed. No trading volume. Market data from companies.json unverified. Company may have financials filed with LuSE SENS but not indexed publicly.
